skill.name: linear-projects-read
skill.description: Read Linear project lists and project details through documented Bun CLI commands.
skill.summary: Read Linear projects from the command line using a Linear API key.
skill.author: Bind

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution mostly come from Markdown command examples, not executable code in the reviewed files. The real risks are legitimate but sensitive use of LINEAR_API_KEY, optional .env loading, and user-run Bun commands that may contact Linear.

medium_findings.title: Linear API Key Access Required
medium_findings.description: The manifest and documentation require LINEAR_API_KEY. This is legitimate for Linear access, but the token is sensitive and must not be logged or exposed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key name is explicit in both the manifest and documentation. I found no evidence that the reviewed files exfiltrate or print the token.

medium_findings.title: Hidden .env Credential Source
medium_findings.description: The documentation allows LINEAR_API_KEY to be set in <git-root>/.env. Hidden environment files commonly store secrets and require careful file permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env credential source is documented directly. The risk is configuration exposure, not confirmed malicious handling.

medium_findings.title: User-Run Bun CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run Bun commands from .opencode/skill paths. These are external commands, but they are examples and not auto-executed by the reviewed files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are visible in Markdown and require user invocation. No executable wrapper or input interpolation was present in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Network False Positive
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the Bun runtime website in a prerequisite list. This is documentation, not a network request from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 15 is a Markdown link only. I found no fetch, curl, or executable network call in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hit at the description line appears to be a substring match. The reviewed line describes read-only operations and contains no cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is front matter text, not code. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm appears in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Heuristic Combination Mostly From Documentation
low_findings.description: The dangerous combination heuristic combines credential text, Markdown commands, and a documentation URL. I did not find prompt injection or obfuscation in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The individual signals are present, but their context is setup documentation and command examples. No hidden instructions or encoded payloads were found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Secret Requirement
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires LINEAR_API_KEY in environment configuration. This should be treated as sensitive credential handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential name and setup instructions are explicit. This is a real sensitive-data pattern with legitimate purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation asks users to execute Bun commands. Command execution is user initiated and should be reviewed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit examples rather than hidden execution. The referenced target scripts are not present in the reviewed file tree.

content.user_title: Read Linear Projects from the CLI
content.value_statement: Teams need quick project visibility without opening the Linear UI. This skill documents read-only CLI commands for listing projects and viewing project details.
actual_capabilities: Lists Linear projects through the documented list-projects command.
Filters project lists by status, project lead, and result limit.
Gets one project by UUID, exact name, or partial name.
Supports table output and JSON output options.
Documents required LINEAR_API_KEY setup for Linear access.
limitations: Requires Bun to be installed before commands can run.
Requires a valid Linear API key with project read access.
Does not create, update, or delete Linear projects.
Reviewed files do not include the referenced command scripts.

use_cases.title: Check Active Projects
use_cases.description: List started Linear projects with a small result limit before a planning meeting.

use_cases.title: Find Projects by Lead
use_cases.description: Filter project lists by lead name to review ownership and workload.

use_cases.title: Inspect One Project
use_cases.description: Fetch details for a project by UUID or partial name during support triage.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Projects
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to list up to 10 Linear projects.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check of available project data.

prompt_templates.title: Filter by Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to list Linear projects with status started and show the normal table output.

prompt_templates.title: Find Lead Ownership
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to list Linear projects led by a named person and return JSON output.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Project Details
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to get the Linear project matching this partial project name, then summarize blockers and status.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced project analysis after command output is available.

output_examples.input: List started Linear projects with a limit of 10.
output_examples.output: A terminal table showing up to 10 projects with started status, including project names and available metadata.

output_examples.input: Get the project named Mount Vernon.
output_examples.output: A project detail view for the matching Linear project, displayed directly from the CLI command.

output_examples.input: List projects led by James Madison and use JSON output.
output_examples.output: A machine-readable project list suitable for filtering, reporting, or follow-up analysis.

best_practices: Use a Linear API key with the minimum permissions needed for project reads.
Store credentials in a local environment file that is ignored by version control.
Use JSON output when another tool must filter or transform the results.
anti_patterns: Do not paste LINEAR_API_KEY into prompts, chat logs, or shared terminals.
Do not use this skill for write operations because it only documents read commands.
Do not assume partial name matching selects the intended project without checking the result.

faq.question: Does this skill modify Linear projects?
faq.answer: No. The documented commands are read-only list and get operations.

faq.question: What credential is required?
faq.answer: It requires LINEAR_API_KEY from Linear personal API key settings.

faq.question: Can it filter projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The list command documents filters for status, project lead, and result limit.

faq.question: Can it return JSON?
faq.answer: Yes. Both documented commands include a JSON output option.

faq.question: Does it require Bun?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation lists the Bun runtime as a prerequisite.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning for API key handling and user-run CLI commands.
